title: Trade Repository (TR) Data Collections
summary: The Bank of England's Trade Repository (TR) Data Collections page describes the regulatory framework for collecting derivatives and securities financing transaction data from UK firms under UK EMIR and UK SFTR, implemented in response to post-2008 financial crisis visibility gaps. The page primarily serves as an informational and guidance resource for reporting counterparties, detailing technical specifications, XML schemas, and validation rules. The latest regulatory changes come into force on 26 January 2026.
key_points: Trade repository data collections were established post-2008/9 financial crisis to address lack of visibility in key financial markets and firm interconnectedness

Data is collected under two regimes: UK European Market Infrastructure Regulation (UK EMIR) for derivatives and UK Securities Financing Transactions Regulation (UK SFTR) for securities financing transactions
UK EMIR covers five asset classes: interest rates, foreign exchange, commodities, credit default swaps, and equities
The data supports the Financial Policy Committee (FPC), Prudential Regulation Committee (PRC), Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), and Financial Market Infrastructure Committee (FMIC)
New UK EMIR reporting requirements come into force on 26 January 2026, with Q&A guidance updated October 2025 and Policy Statement published August 2025
September 2024 saw the revised derivative reporting regime come into force, aligning UK framework with CPMI-IOSCO Technical Guidance on OTC derivatives data harmonisation
why_it_matters: This source describes regulatory infrastructure for financial market transparency rather than providing actionable market intelligence. It is primarily relevant for reporting counterparties (firms subject to UK EMIR/SFTR obligations) and those interested in financial stability data frameworks, not for market participants seeking macro catalysts or market structure insights.
market_structure_context: The source describes data collection frameworks for derivatives and securities financing markets that provide visibility into market structure, but does not itself present market structure analysis or data.
important_numbers: 26 January 2026 (new UK EMIR requirements effective date)
30 September 2024 (revised derivative reporting regime came into force)

notes: This source is NOT relevant to macro catalysts or market structure as those topics are typically understood in finance. It describes the Bank of England's internal data collection framework for regulatory reporting purposes. The source is informational and serves reporting counterparties rather than providing market analysis or economic data that would constitute a macro catalyst. Archived as regulatory guidance document.
